chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to an anonymous repository (https://anonymous.4open.science/r/hash-mvba-7C57/) containing the Python implementation of HMVBA, FIN-MVBA, and sMVBA*, along with experimental scripts.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation was conducted using physical hardware deployment on AWS EC2 t2.medium instances distributed across 13 real-world geographic regions (WAN environment), rather than just simulation or a small testbed.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section III-A contains a clear, explicit definition of the system model and threat assumptions. It formally defines a strongly adaptive adversary corrupting up to f < n/5 nodes in an asynchronous network with authenticated channels.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis of performance trade-offs. Section VII details latency measurements across varying input sizes (L) and network scales (N), comparing HMVBA against FIN-MVBA and sMVBA* to quantify the reduction in communication overhead and resulting latency improvements.

major_weakness: The paper does not include a dedicated section analyzing the computational overhead (CPU usage) or energy consumption of the proposed protocol beyond latency and throughput metrics.
